queue.title: Blog Insights: item page template (sticky side panel, modular body, CTA section)
queue.note: Phase 1. Two-column layout: sticky side panel (category badge, tags, Share & Care, key insights, quote, self-test CTA, related insights) + main content (H1, intro, feature image, modular Rich Text body, De essentie checklist). Bottom CTA section reusable from content template. Desktop + mobile. First version built 2026-04-24.

queue.title: Experiences: item page template (single-column text-only testimonial)
queue.note: Phase 2. H1 (essence of testimonial, max 2 lines) + intro quote + pure text body + subtle author name at end. No images, no embeds. Fully in DOM at load. First version built 2026-04-24.

queue.title: Home: hero header styling and right-side images not matching design
queue.note: Briefing section 4.3 flags large hero header as needing styling + right images. Uses Swiper (not native Webflow slider — w-slider classes in global.css are just resets). Compare against Figma design and fix layout, image placement, and any broken Swiper behaviour. Dev done — still need to edit/replace images.

queue.title: Home: three-image hover sequence (muted-to-colour fade on hover)
queue.note: Phase 4. Section 5.1 Wat als je blijft wachten? — 3 images with muted/lightened default state, hover restores original colour, smooth fade only (no scale/move). Mobile: no hover. After sequence or page load + delay: Maar het hoeft niet zover te komen fades in below images.

queue.title: Home: 123 cards sequential fade-in on scroll into view
queue.note: Phase 4. Section 5.2 — 3 cards fade in sequentially on scroll-into-view. No slide, no scale, just fade. After last card: supporting line fades in. CSS-only or minimal JS with IntersectionObserver.

queue.title: Home: FAQ accordion single-open behaviour (one item open at a time)
queue.note: Phase 4. Section 5.3 — item 01 open by default, one item open at a time (other closes when you open another). +/- icons. All answers in DOM at load, CSS-based collapsing only.

queue.title: Mobile responsive: all pages (Home rebuild from design, all templates aligned)
queue.note: Phase 3. Home mobile is effectively a rebuild from design. Also: NEM Methode, Blog Insights main/category/item, Experiences main/item, Our Mission, Link-in-bio, Blank. Fix nav + notification stacking, typography scale, responsive image sets. 8 hours budgeted.

queue.title: Navigation and footer: wire all links (currently all point to #)
queue.note: Phase 4. Every nav and footer link currently points to #. Wire to correct pages. About/Over submenu points to Our Mission page. Enable NL locale, hide EN.

queue.title: Navigation: subtle shrink-on-scroll sticky behaviour
queue.note: Phase 4. Briefing section 4.2 — nav is prepared/sticky but shrink-on-scroll not implemented. Subtle size reduction on scroll down.

queue.title: Blog Insights: sticky categories bar with in-page anchor navigation on Inzichten page
queue.note: Phase 4. Section 10.1 — horizontal nav below navbar. Need to make it link to in-page anchors on Inzichten page instead of category pages.

queue.title: Blog Insights: CMS wiring (essential, selected, popular, category-based logic)
queue.note: Phase 4. Sections 10.2–10.5. Feature item (Essentieel inzicht, 1 manual toggle). Selected Insights (3 manual). Per-category latest 3 + CTA. Populaire inzichten (3 manual). Also category page logic (11.1–11.7) including tag-based Veel gelezen blocks and load-more.

queue.title: Experiences: journey-type filter, dynamic CTA copy by sex, load-more
queue.note: Phase 4. Section 13. Filter, load-more, dynamic CTA, and hover tint all done.

queue.title: Notification bar: per-page show/hide verification and fix
queue.note: Phase 4. Briefing section 4.2 — notification bar is built but per-page show/hide needs verification. Editable text + CTA, no CLS, no navbar overlap on mobile.

queue.title: Performance, SEO, AEO, accessibility and launch preparation
queue.note: Phase 5. Preload hero, WebP conversion, responsive images. Meta + OG + Article/Organization/FAQPage/Person schema. robots.txt (currently Disallow: /), sitemap, hreflang. GEO/AEO DOM audit. axe-core on every template. Keyboard nav + reduced-motion. Lighthouse baseline. 301 map from TEMP site, DNS cutover, final publish, smoke test, Loom handover. 8 hours budgeted.

queue.title: Read more expand/collapse animation with GSAP and Dutch text toggle
queue.description: GSAP-powered read-more accordion. Button data-button=read-more-expand toggles all sibling data-block=read-more-expand elements. Collapsed by default. Button text swaps Lees meer / Lees minder. Works site-wide.

queue.title: Zelftest: CMS quiz data extraction module for React component
queue.description: Add quiz-data.js module that reads questions and result bands from hidden CMS collection lists and exposes on window.__quizData. React code component consumes via quizDataReady event.

queue.title: 123 cards and wat-als: fix mobile trigger timing and align animations
queue.description: Fix mobile ScrollTrigger start from 'top 85%' to 'bottom 95%' on 123 cards. Rewrite wat-als-reveal.js to use same simple stagger pattern (desktop: opacity+y, mobile: dim-to-bright). Remove hover behaviour.

queue.title: NEM TEST Phase B: full production build (landing page, 5-screen quiz, AI report, backend integration)
queue.description: Phase B of NEM TEST 'Waarom reageer ik zo?' — Webflow AI prompt for React component (5 screens), 2 n8n webhooks (submit, verify), PDF generation, MailerSend + MailerLite, NL+EN i18n. 16.5h total (Will). Alex owns Anthropic prompts. Scoring system TBD.

queue.title: Backend cleanup: translate page names and CMS field labels to English Title Case
queue.description: Translate all Dutch page names to English, ensure slugs are Dutch, rename CMS field displayNames to English Title Case, add missing help texts, flag orphaned fields. Collection-level names require manual Designer rename.

queue.title: NEM Test report delivery: brand the covering email and the PDF report
queue.description: In the /verify n8n workflow (nem-verify.workflow.json), style the two visual surfaces of report delivery. (1) Send Report node (MailerSend): the covering email is currently plain text only — add a branded html body using table-based layout and inline styles so it renders across email clients including Outlook, keeping the existing text as the plain-text fallback. (2) Build HTML node: upgrade the report HTML that PDFShift renders into the attached PDF — add NEM logo header, brand colours, and layout for the mechanism scores, using modern CSS (Chromium engine allows flexbox, web fonts, @page margins). Support NL and EN locales for both surfaces. Re-import the workflow into n8n after editing (or apply live via the n8n MCP if deployed).

queue.title: NEM Test report prompt: make the prompt safe to edit and lift the token limit
queue.description: In the live /verify workflow the Anthropic system prompt is a single-quoted JavaScript string literal inside the Generate Report node's jsonBody expression, so the first apostrophe or newline in Alex's real prompt breaks the node silently (the browser already got its 302). Move the prompt into a Report Prompt Set node as a plain fixed-value string so JSON.stringify handles all escaping. Also lift max_tokens from the live value of 1024 to the intended 8000, switch $json references to explicit $('Validate Token') references, and re-sync the repo workflow JSON to live. Prerequisite for any mechanism that lets the client edit the prompt himself.

queue.title: Report delivery is failing in production: MailerSend is on a trial account
queue.description: Executions 30 and 35 on 2026-07-09 both failed at Send Report with 422 'You have reached trial account unique recipients limit. #MS42225'. Report generation, PDF render and encode all succeed; only delivery fails. Because Mark Consumed sits on the fast path, the token is consumed anyway, so the user sees the confirmed page, never receives a report, and cannot retry. Add To Newsletter also never runs, so consenting users are not subscribed. Blocked on Alex upgrading the MailerSend account off trial. Once unblocked, consider whether Mark Consumed should move back behind successful delivery, and add failure alerting to the report branch so silent failures surface. Corrects the backend README, which currently states MailerSend is production and that no credential work blocks go-live.
